pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 12), export_result.sh (line 6), and verifier.py (line 40)

Task description explicitly states 'Create layers: VISIBLE (White/Black), HIDDEN (Blue, Dashed linetype), DIMENSIONS (Red)'. Verifier.py line 87 confirms req_layers = ['VISIBLE', 'HIDDEN', 'DIMENSIONS']

Metadata claim exists in task.json but is not referenced in verifier.py or any verification logic. Grep search confirms min_entities is not used in any Python verification code. However, 15 entities is mathematically reasonable for a 3-view orthographic drawing (3 rectangular outlines = 12 lines, plus 3+ hidden lines for V-groove and slots, plus 3+ dimension entities would total 18-20 entities).

pi_items.metadata_value: 3.00" Length x 2.50" Width x 2.00" Height with 90-degree V-groove
pi_items.verified_value: Within standard V-block specifications. Standard V-blocks come in sizes including 3" x 1-5/16" x 2-3/8", 6" x 6" x 4", 5" x 4" x 3". The 90-degree angle is the industry standard for V-blocks per metrology standards (IS-2949-1992, IS-4960-1968).
pi_items.source: Web search confirms 90-degree V-blocks are standard specification. Manufacturer datasheets (Bal-tec, Amazon product listings) show similar dimensional ranges.

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data - created by agent during task execution
pi_items.source: Task creates new drawing from scratch in LibreCAD. Setup script (setup_task.sh line 12) deletes any previous output file. No reference data or template is used. Unlike other LibreCAD tasks in this environment (draw_rectangle, etc.) which use the downloaded floorplan.dxf, orthographic_vblock generates new content.

privileged_info_summary: The orthographic_vblock task generates synthetic data—a new 3-view orthographic technical drawing created in LibreCAD and saved as a DXF file to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/vblock_projection.dxf. The task requires three drawing layers (VISIBLE, HIDDEN, DIMENSIONS) representing visible lines, hidden construction lines, and dimensions. The V-Block part specifications (3.00" length × 2.50" width × 2.00" height with 90-degree V-groove) conform to standard metrology V-block specifications per industry standards (IS-2949-1992, IS-4960-1968). The metadata claims a minimum of 15 entities in the drawing, but this constraint is not enforced by the verification code; however, 15 entities is a reasonable minimum for a 3-view orthographic drawing with hidden lines and dimensions.
